data.status: opening for the season this Saturday
data.short_description: Rose Dairy, a seasonal ice cream and burger stand originally established in 1951, will reopen for the season on Saturday, March 28th at 906 South Main Street in Cheshire. The roadside stand serves hand-pressed charbroiled hamburgers, hot dogs, milkshakes, soft serve and hard serve ice cream, and lobster rolls. The business operates seasonally from March until November.

data.location: 50 Providence Pike, Putnam, CT (former Big Lots space)
data.short_description: Bounce X Fun Zone is under construction at 50 Providence Pike in Putnam, occupying the remainder of the former Big Lots space next to Montana Nights Axe Throwing. The children's entertainment complex will feature trampolines, a multi-sports court, climbing structures, arcade games, a Ninja warrior course, a toddler area, and two party rooms. No opening date has been announced.

The Starbucks location planned for the new TownePlace & Fairfield Inn & Suites at 363 Roberts Street in East Hartford appears to be cancelled, as the 2,650 sq ft space has been listed for lease. The Connecticut Scoop reached out to Starbucks and the broker for confirmation but received no response. The space is described as a quick-service drive-thru restaurant opportunity.

data.location: 1054 Chapel Street, New Haven, CT (former Avocado Haven)
data.short_description: 7th Street Burger is coming soon to 1054 Chapel Street in Downtown New Haven, taking over the former Avocado Haven space. The chain specializes in smash burgers, loaded fries, and late-night bites and currently has dozens of locations across New York, Washington DC, Massachusetts, Maryland, New Jersey, and Pennsylvania. Signs have recently been installed on the exterior of the new space.

data.location: 1483 Pleasant Valley Road, Manchester, CT (former Hooters)
data.short_description: Shogun X Revolving Sushi is planning a soft opening at 1483 Pleasant Valley Road in Manchester, the former Hooters location, pending final town approvals. The all-you-can-eat restaurant will feature a conveyor belt system delivering sushi, sashimi, and other Japanese favorites to tables. A grand opening date has not yet been announced.

data.location: 22 Raymond Road, West Hartford, CT (former Toro Mexican Street Food)
data.short_description: Mozz & Co., an upscale Italian delicatessen, will open on April 8th at 22 Raymond Road in West Hartford, occupying the former Toro Mexican Street Food space. The menu includes chicken cutlets, porchetta, broccoli rabe, house-made meats, salads, desserts, and espresso, with Italian dinner service on Saturdays and Sundays. The business is owned by chef Joey Cusano, formerly of Le Mazet.

data.location: Brass Mill Center, 495 Union Street, Waterbury, CT (food court, second level)
data.status: set opening date at Brass Mill Center
data.short_description: Cinnabon will open at Brass Mill Center's food court at 495 Union Street in Waterbury on Tuesday, March 31st at 12:00pm, marking the brand's return to the mall after operating there until early 2025. The bakery chain specializes in cinnamon rolls, coffees, Chillattas, and other sweets. It will share a space with Auntie Anne's Pretzels.

data.location: Brass Mill Center, 495 Union Street, Waterbury, CT (food court, second level)
data.status: set opening date at Brass Mill Center
data.short_description: Auntie Anne's Pretzels will open alongside Cinnabon in a shared food court space at Brass Mill Center on March 31st at 12:00pm, representing a new tenant for the mall. The chain serves soft-baked pretzels, pretzel dogs, lemonades, and other items. A similar pretzel franchise, Pretzelmaker, previously operated at the mall.

data.location: 2505 Main Street, Stratford, CT (former Olde Dog Tavern, Station House Square)
data.status: expected to open during the summer
data.short_description: Deep 6 Arcade will open at 2505 Main Street in Stratford, taking over the former Olde Dog Tavern space directly across from the Stratford Train Station. The venue will combine a restaurant, bar, and arcade featuring carefully restored classic machines from former Connecticut arcades, including Pac-Man, Galaga, Tempest, and pinball games. It will also offer live music, private event rooms, and arcade game sales and repair.

data.location: 25 Falls Road, Moodus section, East Haddam, CT (former Pizzeria da Vinci)
data.status: planned to open in late spring
data.short_description: Thena Pita Bar will open at 25 Falls Road in the Moodus section of East Haddam, occupying the former Pizzeria da Vinci space near HomeTown Market at The Old Lumberyard. The restaurant will serve authentic Greek street food including gyros, souvlaki, and baklava, and will be owned by Niko Papathanasiou and Sarah Turker, who have operated the Greekin' Out food truck since 2015. No exact opening date has been set.

non_working.reason: Real estate listing article — no business opening or closure event; Avery's Soda relocation already occurred in 2025 prior to this article
Cancelled opening plan — no confirmed opening or closing, space listed for lease with no new tenant confirmed
